title: pdf-evidence learning candidate (Train-on-Use v0.3.0)
description: A raw signal captured during RUN mode that the skill detected something worth learning (new document type, citation defect, table uncertainty, OCR gap, user-reported error, novel pattern). Candidates are NOT patches and NOT memory entries — they are inputs for future TRAIN sessions. See policies.md#train-on-use.

candidate_id.description: Format: LC-YYYY-NNN (sequential within a year). Never reused.

skill_version.description: Skill version at the time the candidate was captured.

source_session.description: RUN-mode session id or task id where the candidate was detected. Empty if ad-hoc.

input_file_type.description: Brief descriptor of input (e.g. 'PDF — financial report EN', 'PDF — legal VI'). Do NOT store sensitive file content.

document_type_detected.description: The doc_type the classify step returned (one of the v0.2.0 enum, or 'unknown_<signal>' if it fell outside).

trigger.description: Which detection rule fired (see policies.md#train-on-use triggers).

observed_issue.description: Short factual description of what was detected. NO sensitive content from the file.

evidence.description: Optional verbatim snippet (≤300 chars) proving the issue, e.g. a malformed citation or a parser warning. Skip if it would leak sensitive content.

risk_level.description: Risk if this issue recurs unchecked: high = hallucination/legal/financial harm; medium = citation/table defect; low = cosmetic/UX.

suggested_action.description: What should happen next. ignore = noise; add_memory = trivial lesson; create_regression = lock a behavior; train_session = needs full loop; proposal_required = needs human + minor/major bump.

related_failure_id.description: If the candidate maps to a known F-NN in 06-FAILURE_MODES.md or 20-FAILURE_MODES-style id, name it. Else null.

requires_human_review.description: true when suggested_action ∈ {train_session, proposal_required} OR risk_level=high.

status.description: Lifecycle. RUN mode only creates 'open'. TRAIN/RELEASE may transition it.
